lines: A stone set low in the wall of the westmost cell lifts free on a fingernail. Behind it, dry where nothing else is, sits a flat tin the size of a prayer book.

Inside is a heavy iron key, oiled against rust, and a scrap folded small. 'For the wall box. There is no pay in it worth dying for. I left it for whoever outlives this place, not for the diocese. They can dig their own holes.'
You pocket the key. It is cold and good in the hand, the way old iron is.
